chapter: Forgetting Why You Entered a Room
latin: Celeriter a culina surrexi quia aliquid magni momenti in cubiculo erat. cum intravissem, omnino oblitus sum cur venissem. in culina rursus sedi. tum — recordatus sum! sed iterum oblitus sum in itinere.

questions.prompt: What happens when the narrator enters the room?
questions.target: He completely forgets why he came
choices: He completely forgets why he came
He gets distracted by his phone

questions.target: He forgets again on the way
choices: He forgets again on the way

translation: I quickly got up from the kitchen because something very important was in the bedroom. When I entered, I completely forgot why I had come. I sat in the kitchen again. Then — I remembered! But I forgot again on the way.
